{{ill|Sakai clan|en|Sakai clan|WD=Q2975346|lt=Sakai Clan|1=Sakai clan}} (Warrior Family & Aristocracy (Count))
{{ill|Ashizawa Clan|de|Ashizawa‐Clan|ja|芦澤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizawa Clan|1=Q16482553|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482553}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Echigo Hojo Clan|de|Echigo‐Hojo‐Clan|ja|越後北条氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Hojo Clan|1=Kitajō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636498}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Yasuda clan|en|Yasuda clan|WD=Q8049951|lt=Yasuda Clan|1=Yasuda clan}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Yamamura Clan|de|Yamamura‐Clan|ja|山村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

{{ill|Echigo Hojo Clan|de|Echigo‐Hojo‐Clan|ja|越後北条氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Hojo Clan|1=Kitajō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636498}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Yasuda clan|en|Yasuda clan|WD=Q8049951|lt=Yasuda Clan|1=Yasuda clan}} (Warrior Family)

{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==

Kyoto Prefecture {{ill|Ooe Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|de|Ooe‐Dorf (Kyoto)|ja|大枝村 (京都府)|lt=Ooe Village|lt_ja=大枝村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Ooe|de|Ooe|ja|京都市西京区の町名#旧大枝村、大原野村|lt=Ooe|lt_ja=大枝|lt_de=Ooe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe|1=Ooe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375126}}, ({{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}}}}) Kyoto City {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, ({{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}}}) Kyoto City {{ill|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1154588|lt=Nishikyō Ward|1=Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto}} Ooe District{{ill|Ōeyama (mountain)|en|Ōeyama (mountain)|WD=Q3390824|lt=Ooe Mountain|1=Ōeyama (mountain)}} (Ooe Mountain, Ooe Slope, Old Slope, Oi Mountain) |Notable Figures = {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}}

{{ill|Echigo Hojo Clan|de|Echigo‐Hojo‐Clan|ja|越後北条氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Hojo Clan|1=Kitajō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11636498}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Yasuda clan|en|Yasuda clan|WD=Q8049951|lt=Yasuda Clan|1=Yasuda clan}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Yamamura Clan|de|Yamamura‐Clan|ja|山村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Warrior Family)
{{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|WD=Q1872031|lt=Asai Clan|1=Azai clan}} (Warrior Family){{Sfn|Sasakawa|2004|p=46-48}}, etc. }} '''Ooe Clan''' (大江氏) is a clan whose name is derived from “Ooe.” Its surname ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is of courtier origin. It has been a noble family from ancient to early modern Japan. “Ooe” is a surname ({{ill|Honsei|de|Honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}}) and not a family name ({{ill|Myōji|de|Myōji|ja|苗字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名字|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}), similar to clans such as {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|WD=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}}. == Nara Period == {{ill|Ancient history|en|Ancient history|WD=Q41493|lt=Ancient|1=Ancient history}} {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} of the {{ill|Haji clan|en|Haji clan|WD=Q10927758|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Clan|1=Haji clan}} is considered the origin. In the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (791, the 10th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} – {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}), relatives of {{ill|Ooe Shorogami|de|Ooe Shorogami|ja|大枝諸上|lt=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|lt_ja=土師諸上|lt_de=Tsuchi‐haji Shorogami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Shorogami|1=Ōe no Morogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436225}} were granted the surname '''Ooe'''. == Heian Period == In October of {{ill|866|en|866|WD=Q23872|lt=866|1=866}} ({{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Seigan|1=Jōgan}} 8), {{ill|Ōe no Otondo|en|Ōe no Otondo|WD=Q11436894|lt=Ooe Otohito|1=Ōe no Otondo}} changed his surname from '''Ooe Shorogami''' to '''Ooe''' via a {{ill|Name Change|de|Namensänderung|ja|改姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Name Change|1=family name change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498856}}. The rationale was that if a branch ({{ill|Branch|en|Branch|WD=Q2923673|lt=Branch|1=Branch}}) became too large, it might cause the trunk ({{ill|Trunk|de|Stamm|ja|幹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Trunk|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 樹幹|1=Trunk (botany)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q193472}}) of the main family to break (a case of overthrow), which was seen as inauspicious. However, since the Ooe surname had been bestowed by Emperor Kanmu, a complete change was not possible; only the kanji notation was altered while retaining the pronunciation. In addition, the name “Ooe” is said to signify that the family will prosper endlessly like a great river (江).''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'' Seigan 8, October 15 The Ooe Clan produced many distinguished poets and scholars, and they were held in high esteem by the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court|1=Royal court}}. Among the “Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals” ({{ill|Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|de|Mittlere Sechsunddreißig Dichter|ja|中古三十六歌仙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Thirty‐Six Poetic Immortals|1=Thirty Six Elder Poetic Sages|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1216201}}) – a selection of the era’s foremost poets – were chosen from the Ooe Clan: {{ill|Ōe no Chisato|en|Ōe no Chisato|WD=Q3074985|lt=Ooe Senri|1=Ōe no Chisato}}, {{ill|Ooe Masakane|de|Ooe Masakane|ja|大江匡衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Masakane|1=Ōe no Masahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2395120}}, {{ill|Ooe Kagen|de|Ooe Kagen|ja|大江嘉言|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooe Kagen|1=Ōe no Yoshitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594014}}, and among women, {{ill|Izumi Shikibu|en|Izumi Shikibu|WD=Q239898|lt=Izumi Shikibu|1=Izumi Shikibu}}, and {{ill|Akazome Emon|en|Akazome Emon|WD=Q269755|lt=Akazome Emon|1=Akazome Emon}} (wife of Masakane). In addition, a great scholar of the Heian period—descended from Ooe Masafusa—is said to have taught {{ill|Military Tactics|de|Militärtaktik|ja|兵法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Tactics|1=Q11393678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393678}} to Minamoto no Yoshie (Yahata Tarō) of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}. == Later Heian Period == In the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|WD=Q7063|lt=11th Century|1=11th century}}, a fixed hierarchy was established with the Fujiwara clan at its apex (via the regency system {{ill|Five regent houses|en|Five regent houses|WD=Q1067142|lt=Kampaku|1=Five regent houses}}), and the Ooe Clan was Ranked at the Fifth Court Rank, serving as regional governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kunshi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) in local areas—thus placing them among the middle to lower nobility. Moreover, as they also functioned as scholars in the family school ({{ill|Family School|de|Familienschule|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family School|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}}), they produced a number of literary aristocrats ({{ill|Literary Aristocracy|de|Literarischer Adel|ja|文人貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Literary Aristocracy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).Overview of Japanese Intellectual History (Hiroshi Ichikawa) == Kamakura Period == In {{ill|1184|en|1184|WD=Q19728|lt=1184|1=1184}} (the first year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}}) the head of the {{ill|Kawachi Genji|en|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652|lt=Kawachi Minamoto Clan|1=Kawachi Genji}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, employed {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Ooe Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, a great‐grandson of Ooe Masafusa, and supported his rise through administrative reforms. When Yoritomo established the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, Hiromoto ascended to the shogunate’s inner circle and acquired vast estates. By distributing these lands among his children, he became the progenitor of the warrior branch of the Ooe Clan, which includes, among others, the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}. == Later Period == [[File:Ichimonjimitsuboshi.png|thumb|right|100px|Ichimonji Triple Star (Nagato Star)]] The descendants of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} flourished as warriors in various regions. Among his descendants are the following. In addition, the descendant of Mōri Katsumitsu, the fourth son, from the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|WD=Q914637|lt=Mōri Clan|1=Mōri clan}}, settled in Yoshida Manor of {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, and from that bloodline emerged the Sengoku daimyo {{ill|Mōri Motonari|en|Mōri Motonari|WD=Q1156545|lt=Mōri Motonari|1=Mōri Motonari}} and the Toyotomi Shogunate’s Five Elders, including {{ill|Mōri Terumoto|en|Mōri Terumoto|WD=Q1142657|lt=Mōri Terumoto|1=Mōri Terumoto}}. Many families adopted the family crest “Three Stars in One Character,” and numerous branches used the characters {{'''{{ill|Hiro|en|Hiro|WD=Q402348|lt=Hiro|1=Hiro}}'''}} and {{'''{{ill|Moto|en|Moto|WD=Q527891|lt=Moto|1=Moto}}'''}} as their “reading characters” ({{ill|Tōoriji|de|Tōoriji|ja|通字|lt=Tōoriji|lt_ja=通字|lt_de=Tōoriji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōoriji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諱|1=Taboo name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q64026473}}). == Genealogy ==
